learningObjectives.title: Understand freight orders
learningObjectives.description: Explain freight order as the primary execution document created after planning.

learningObjectives.title: Understand freight order lifecycle
learningObjectives.description: Describe status progression from creation through execution and settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate FO and FU
learningObjectives.description: Clarify planning demand versus execution document roles.

sections.content: A Freight Order (FO) is the primary execution document in SAP Transportation Management. After transportation planning is completed, SAP TM creates Freight Orders that contain all information required to execute a shipment, including assigned carrier, transportation resources, schedule, locations and transportation stages.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Freight Orders execute transportation. Freight Units represent transportation demand, while Freight Orders represent transportation execution.

A transportation planner consolidates eight Freight Units into one Freight Order for deliveries from Hyderabad to Bengaluru. SAP TM assigns the preferred carrier, allocates a 20-ton truck, schedules pickup and delivery, and releases the Freight Order for execution.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Release Freight Orders only after validating carrier assignment, resource availability and transportation schedules to avoid execution issues.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A retail company creates approximately 6,500 Freight Orders every day. Each Freight Order is automatically generated by the optimizer, assigned to a preferred carrier and monitored until freight settlement.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: One of the most common SAP TM interview questions is: 'What is the difference between a Freight Unit and a Freight Order?' Remember: Freight Unit = Planning Demand, Freight Order = Execution Document.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not create Freight Orders manually for every shipment. In most SAP TM implementations, Freight Orders are generated automatically through transportation planning.

items: Freight Order is the primary transportation execution document.
Freight Orders are created from planned Freight Units.
They contain carrier, resource and schedule information.
Freight Orders progress through execution status changes.
They are used for transportation execution and freight settlement.
